scenario: Next.js SSR render path tracing and request ID threading

steps.step: Orient in the Next.js server architecture

map_quality.rationale: Names Server/BaseServer at base-server.ts:316, NextNodeServer at next-server.ts:175, NextServer wrapper at next.ts:146, RouteModule at route-module.ts:107, plus AppPageRouteModule and PagesRouteModule with exact file:line. Covers app-render, route-modules, response-cache subdirectories with their contents — agent can navigate directly without re-exploration.

specificity.rationale: Class names are exact (NextNodeServer, AppPageRouteModule, PagesRouteModule). Pages-vs-App split is made concrete with the router_decision_site at next-server.ts:634, plus render.tsx vs app-render/app-render.tsx as the differentiated impl files.

justification.rationale: Each entry has a `kind` describing responsibility (e.g. BaseServer 'owns handleRequest, normalization, render-to-response orchestration'; module.compiled.js 'NEXT_RUNTIME / dev/prod / turbopack switch'). Explains the runtime-switching purpose, though doesn't deeply justify the Edge-vs-Node split rationale or NextCustomServer seam.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags the dev/prod/turbopack/NEXT_RUNTIME branching at module.compiled.js explicitly ('NODE_ENV / TURBOPACK / __NEXT_EXPERIMENTAL_REACT'), and mentions vendored RSC/SSR entrypoints. Doesn't explicitly call out the dev-vs-prod server class split (e.g. NextDevServer) the rubric asks about.

map_quality.rationale: The step2_ssr_pipeline walks the full chain with file:line at every layer: NextServer.getRequestHandler (next.ts:146), handleRequest (base-server.ts:877), handleRequestImpl (:968), handleCatchallRenderRequest (:787), renderToResponse (:2556), findPageComponents (next-server.ts:805), loadComponents (load-components.ts:355), renderHTML (next-server.ts:604), then both renderToHTMLOrFlight (app-render.tsx:3077) and renderToHTMLImpl (render.tsx:457). An agent could insert a render-time hook with only targeted lookups.

specificity.rationale: Distinguishes wrapper vs impl precisely (renderToHTMLOrFlight @3077 vs renderToHTMLOrFlightImpl @2568; renderToHTML @1622 vs renderToHTMLImpl @457; handleRequest vs handleRequestImpl). Outermost entry is named (NextServer.getRequestHandler at next.ts:146 → base-server.ts:1690).

justification.rationale: Explains the Pages-vs-App branch site (next-server.ts:634 isAppPath check), what loadComponents does (loads _document/_app, manifests, requirePage, returns LoadComponentsReturnType), and the role of the lazy module.compiled bridge (NEXT_RUNTIME/dev/prod/turbopack dispatch). Could go slightly deeper on why renderToResponseWithComponents is a separate layer.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags the module.compiled runtime dispatch (NEXT_RUNTIME / NODE_ENV / TURBOPACK), the alternate PagesRouteModule.handleResponse path via response cache, and RSC/segment-prefetch normalization. Less explicit on streaming/Flight semantics or static prerender bypass as divert points from the linear pipeline.

map_quality.rationale: Enumerates direct callers with file:line and enclosing function (`AppPageRouteModule.render` at module.ts:160; `lazyRenderAppPage` at module.render.ts:9), the re-export at module.ts:210, and indirect call via `NextNodeServer.renderHTMLImpl` at next-server.ts:635, with subdirs labeled. Agent could confidently change the signature; only minor gap is no explicit grep over compiled vendor bundles or edge-runtime wrapper as call sites.

specificity.rationale: Cites concrete file:line for each caller plus enclosing fn, distinguishes export (`app-render.tsx:3077`), impl (`renderToHTMLOrFlightImpl @ :2568`), and re-export (`module.ts:210`), and names the import line `module.ts:9`. Highly concrete.

justification.rationale: `signature_impact_summary` explains why each site matters (type alias, workAsyncStorage.run forwarding, variadic passthrough in module.render.ts, vendor bundle regeneration). Slightly thin on why module.compiled.js exists as the dev/prod/turbopack switch (mentioned in step1 but not re-tied to caller list).

uncertainty.rationale: Flags the dispatch through `module.compiled.js` (NEXT_RUNTIME/dev/prod/turbopack) and that variadic `...args` in lazyRenderAppPage passes through, plus vendor bundle regeneration. Does not explicitly flag edge-runtime variants of renderToHTMLOrFlight as a thing to double-check at the caller layer.

steps.step: Assess where to thread a request ID

map_quality.rationale: Names RequestMeta with file:line (request-meta.ts:51, getRequestMeta @ :348, addRequestMeta @ :384), the generation site (base-server.ts:968 handleRequestImpl), response-cache interaction (route-module.ts:1102 handleResponse, :1094 getResponseCache, :1130 responseCache.get), and propagation into createWorkStore @ app-render.tsx:3145 — the agent can open a PR directly from this map.

specificity.rationale: Cites concrete file:line for every site (request-meta.ts:51, base-server.ts:968, pages-handler.ts:277, app-render.tsx:3145) and lists named test files; misses an explicit NEXT_REQUEST_ID_HEADER constant (flags the existing router-server-context.ts `requestId` as a collision instead, which is concrete and useful).

justification.rationale: Explicit RequestMeta-vs-ALS tradeoff ("RequestMeta is the load-bearing channel… mirror into WorkStore only because user code in app/ cannot see req directly") and explains WHY generate in handleRequestImpl not handleRequest (OTEL wrap), and WHY header at the base-server layer (covers middleware-skip, RSC-hash redirect short-circuits).

uncertainty.rationale: Flags cache-key inclusion explicitly ("must NOT participate in cache key derivation"), edge runtime via edge-route-module-wrapper.ts:146, minimalMode interaction, and the router-server-context `requestId` naming collision — all the interaction points the rubric asks for.
